solution: Answer & Solution Answer: Option C Solution: This royal house, which stands in the Capital city Agartala covering one sq.Km. area was built by Maharaja Radha Kishore Manikya during 1899-1901.It is a two storied mansion, having a mixed type of architecture with three high domes, the central one being 86′ high.

question: In which year was the concept of "Tribal Sub-Plan" adopted in Tripura
solution: Answer & Solution Answer: Option C Solution: "Tribal Sub-Plan" was adopted in Tripura in 1974. Tribal Sub-Plan came into existence in 1974 - 75 as a strategy for the development of areas having tribal concentration. After merger of Plan and Non-Plan, the TSP was renamed as Scheduled Tribe Component (STC) by Ministry of Finance. 41 Central Ministries / Departments have been identified for earmarking of STC.

question: Who was the first olympian from Tripura
solution: Answer & Solution Answer: Option B Solution: Saraswati Dey is the first olympian from Tripura. Saraswati Dey-Saha (born 23rd November 1979) is an Indian former track and field sprinter from Chittamara, Belonia Tripura. She holds the current 200 metres national record of 22.82 seconds set at the National Circuit Athletic Meet held in Ludhiana on 28th August 2002.

question: Where is the modern psychiatric hospital of Tripura located?

question: Who was the husband of Queen Bhanumati of Tripura?
solution: Answer & Solution Answer: Option A Solution: Maharani Bhanumati was most favourite Queen of Maharaja Bir Chandra Manikya. She was the daughter of Rajkumar Ningthem Kulendra of Kasba (Keilashgar) and sister of Rajkumar Ranadhwaj who was one of the influential ministers of Bir Chandra Manikya. Maharani Bhanumati gave birth to prince Samarendra who was appointed as Barthakur. When the King was in a state of shock following the untimely death of Maharani Bhanumati Devi in the year 1881, he studied young Rabindra Nath Tagore's celebrated love-poem 'Bhagna Hriday'. Bir Chandra found a resonance of his anguish in Tagore's elegiac lyric and sent his political Secretary to Jorasanko near Calcutta to congratulate the poet on his behalf.

question: Folk Dance of Tripura
solution: Answer & Solution Answer: Option B Solution: The main folk dances of Tripura are - Hozagiri dance of Reang community, Garia, Jhum, Maimita, Masak Sumani and Lebang boomani dances of Tripuri community, Bijhu dance of Chakma community, Cheraw and Welcome dances of Lusai community, Hai-Hak dance of Malsum community, Wangala dance of Garo Community, Sangraiaka, Chimithang, Padisha and Abhangma dances of Mog community, Garia dances of Kalai and Jamatia communities, Gajan, Dhamail Sari and Rabindra dances of Bengali community and Basanta Rash and Pung chalam dances of Manipuri community.

question: Who was the first Governor of Tripura
solution: Answer & Solution Answer: Option A Solution: B. K. Nehru was the first governor of Tripura. He was Governor of Jammu and Kashmir (1981 - 84), Assam (1968 - 73), Gujarat (1984 - 86), Nagaland (1968 - 73), Meghalaya (1970 - 73), Manipur (1972 - 73) and Tripura (1972 - 73).

question: The Clouded Leopard National Park is located at?
solution: Answer & Solution Answer: Option D Solution: Clouded Leopard National Park is a national park in the Sepahijala Wildlife Sanctuary Tripura, India. question: Who built the Bhubaneswari temple in Tripura?
solution: Answer & Solution Answer: Option A Solution: Bhubaneswari temple is a Hindu temple located in Tripura, India, in Rajnagar (55 km from Agartala). Believed to be built in 1660 - 1675 AD, it is located next to the palace built by Maharaja Govinda Manikya. The temple is mentioned in Rabindranath Tagore's work.

solution: Answer & Solution Answer: Option A Solution: The first railway train (metre gauge) rolled into Dharmanagar station of North Tripura from Kalkalighat in Karimganj district of Assam in 1964. After that there was a long pause.

question: Garia Puja is performed in the month of-
solution: Answer & Solution Answer: Option B Solution: Garia Puja is a very important festival in Tripura celebrated by the tribal communities. It's a harvest festival linked to the agricultural cycle. Baisakh (which usually falls in April-May) is the month when Garia Puja is traditionally performed. The other options are incorrect because: Chaitra (usually March-April) is just *before* Baisakh, and not when Garia Puja happens. Magha (usually January-February) is much earlier in the year, so it is not related to Garia Puja time. So, remember Garia Puja = Baisakh month!
